subject: DevOps
title: Managed Kubernetes Services
description: A quick win: understand Managed Kubernetes Services.

hook.text: Running Kubernetes yourself is complex. EKS, AKS, and GKE manage the control plane so you don't.

buildup.text: Managed K8s handles master nodes, etcd, API server upgrades, and control plane uptime. You run pods.

discovery.text: GKE Autopilot goes further: Google manages control plane and workers. You just deploy containers.

twist.text: Managed doesn't mean zero work. You still configure networking, RBAC, policies, and monitoring.

climax.text: EKS, AKS, and GKE plug into their cloud's IAM, networking, and storage for smooth operations.

punchline.text: Managed K8s: less cluster toil, more app focus.

quiz.question: What does a managed Kubernetes service handle for you?
options: The control plane: master nodes, etcd, API server, and upgrades
Your application code and business logic
The entire security configuration with no input needed
